summary: Stuart Russell and fellow AI researchers advocate for a ban on offensive autonomous weapons beyond meaningful human control. They warn that initiating an AI arms race will inevitably lead to the mass production of cheap, ubiquitous killing machines that will proliferate to terrorists, dictators, and warlords.

principles.statement: The primary goal of AI research should be to benefit humanity.
principles.rationale: AI has immense potential to improve society, and researchers must ensure their work provides societal benefits rather than just rushing to build the most powerful machines.
principles.source_quote: AI has great potential to benefit humanity in many ways, and that the goal of the field should be to do so.

principles.statement: A global AI arms race must be prevented at all costs.
principles.rationale: If any major military power develops AI weapons, an arms race becomes inevitable, leading to the mass production of cheap, ubiquitous weapons that will fall into the hands of bad actors.
principles.source_quote: If any major military power pushes ahead with AI weapon development, a global arms race is virtually inevitable

principles.statement: Offensive autonomous weapons beyond meaningful human control should be banned.
principles.rationale: These weapons lower the threshold for going to battle and are ideal for assassinations, destabilizing nations, and ethnic cleansing.
principles.source_quote: Starting a military AI arms race is a bad idea, and should be prevented by a ban on offensive autonomous weapons beyond meaningful human control.

mental_models.name: The Kalashnikovs of Tomorrow
mental_models.description: A lens for understanding the proliferation of autonomous weapons. Unlike nuclear weapons, which require rare and costly materials, AI weapons rely on software and mass-produced hardware, meaning they will inevitably become cheap, ubiquitous, and easily accessible to non-state actors.
mental_models.example: Comparing the trajectory of AI weapons to nuclear arms to highlight that AI weapons will not remain tightly controlled strategic assets, but will instead flood the black market.

mental_models.name: The Third Revolution in Warfare
mental_models.description: Categorizing the advent of autonomous weapons as a fundamental paradigm shift in human conflict, on par with the invention of gunpowder and nuclear arms.
mental_models.example: Using this historical framing to emphasize the high stakes and urgency of banning autonomous weapons before they are deployed.

signature_quotes.text: autonomous weapons have been described as the third revolution in warfare, after gunpowder and nuclear arms.
signature_quotes.context: Describing the stakes and scale of the threat posed by AI weapon systems.

signature_quotes.text: If any major military power pushes ahead with AI weapon development, a global arms race is virtually inevitable, and the endpoint of this technological trajectory is obvious: autonomous weapons will become the Kalashnikovs of tomorrow.
signature_quotes.context: Warning about the inevitable proliferation of AI weapons if an arms race begins.

signature_quotes.text: Unlike nuclear weapons, they require no costly or hard-to-obtain raw materials, so they will become ubiquitous and cheap for all significant military powers to mass-produce.
signature_quotes.context: Explaining why autonomous weapons are uniquely dangerous regarding proliferation.

signature_quotes.text: Starting a military AI arms race is a bad idea, and should be prevented by a ban on offensive autonomous weapons beyond meaningful human control.
signature_quotes.context: The core call to action in the open letter.

anti_patterns.description: Pushing ahead with military AI weapon development to gain a strategic advantage.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It inevitably triggers a global arms race, resulting in cheap, mass-produced autonomous weapons that will end up on the black market and be used by terrorists and warlords against civilian populations.

anti_patterns.description: Allowing the field of AI to be tarnished by the development of autonomous weapons.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It risks creating a major public backlash against artificial intelligence, which would curtail the technology's future societal benefits.
